001.png:
his purpose effectually, even brilliantly. Even as it
was, there had been something very striking about the
manner in which he had fought his way from the far
southern coasts to Yorktown.
002.png:
Lord North knew what the news meant when it came.
He received it as he would have taken a ball in his
breast, opening his arms and exclaiming wildly,
O God! it is all over!
003.png:
France and Spain had taken advantage of the revolt
of the colonies once more to attack her, not because
they loved America or sympathized with the ideals of
liberty which the colonists professed.
004.png:
The war had gone hard with England for four years.
She had lost army after army in America, and the
whole world had watched the struggle with mingled
wonder and apprehension at the tenacity of the fight.
005.png:
Washington had handled his forces with consummate
skill and patience throughout the long campaigns.
He was not a brilliant general in the European sense,
but no other man could have held the army together.
006.png:
The Constitution was the work of practical men who
understood the weakness of government under the
Articles of Confederation and were determined to
build something that would endure and prevail.
007.png:
Madison had come to Philadelphia with a scheme already
formed in his mind, the outline of a national government
which should act directly upon individuals and not
merely upon states as such.
008.png:
The new government found its greatest difficulty in
the lack of trained administrative officers and the
absence of any tradition of Federal authority to which
men might appeal when local interests clashed.
